name: friction tracker writes one JSON-line per event so downstream consumers can parse without state-machine awareness
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro/friction && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/scripts/friction-tracker.sh" --event hook-block --hook x --decision deny --at 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z 2>err.txt && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/hooks/scripts/friction-tracker.sh" --event hook-block --hook y --decision deny --at 2026-05-13T00:00:01Z 2>err.txt && [ "$(wc -l < .claude-tdd-pro/friction/events.jsonl)" -eq 2 ] && node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('.claude-tdd-pro/friction/events.jsonl','utf8').split('\n')[0])" && echo ok 1>&2
